title: Airline Fuel Surcharge Announcement Spike
thesis: Spikes in airline surcharge announcements signal crude oil pressure and margin compression in carriers, triggering flight capacity cuts and reduced travel demand—bearish for discretionary travel and hospitality. Fuel surcharge announcements signal rising operational costs, capacity constraint, and reduced pricing power—reducing leisure travel demand.
data_sources: RSS feed count for 'airline fuel surcharge', 'airline ticket price increase fuel', 'carrier surcharge announcement' via rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for surcharge announcements exceeds 8 for 2 consecutive days, short XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for surcharge announcements exceeds 8 for 2 consecutive days, short XLY Exit: After 2 weeks or if daily count falls below 4 for 7 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count for 'airline fuel surcharge', 'airline ticket price increase fuel', 'carrier surcharge announcement' via rss_count adapter via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Oil price volatility and quarterly fare review cycles trigger surcharge announcement waves multiple times per year; seasonal travel demand spikes force pricing announcement clusters.

data.source: RSS feed count for 'airline fuel surcharge', 'airline ticket price increase fuel', 'carrier surcharge announcement' via rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in airline surcharge announcements signal crude oil pressure and margin compression in carriers, triggering flight capacity cuts and reduced travel demand—bearish for discretionary travel and hospitality.

market_impact.rationale: Fuel surcharge announcements signal rising operational costs, capacity constraint, and reduced pricing power—reducing leisure travel demand.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS count for surcharge announcements exceeds 8 for 2 consecutive days, short XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 2 weeks or if daily count falls below 4 for 7 consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Oil price volatility and quarterly fare review cycles trigger surcharge announcement waves multiple times per year; seasonal travel demand spikes force pricing announcement clusters.

title: Restaurant Expansion Announcement Clustering
thesis: Clusters of restaurant expansion announcements signal optimistic consumer outlook, real estate availability, and labor market confidence—bullish signals for consumer discretionary and hospitality. Restaurant expansion waves signal consumer confidence, foot traffic expectations, and real estate market health.
data_sources: RSS feed count for 'restaurant chain opening', 'new restaurant location announcement', 'restaurant franchise expansion' via rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for restaurant expansion announcements exceeds 10 for 3 consecutive days, go long XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for restaurant expansion announcements exceeds 10 for 3 consecutive days, go long XLY Exit: After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count falls below 6 for 5 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count for 'restaurant chain opening', 'new restaurant location announcement', 'restaurant franchise expansion' via rss_count adapter via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Quarterly earnings announcements and franchise disclosure documents release expansion plans in clusters; seasonal tourism and holiday planning drive announcement waves regularly.

data.source: RSS feed count for 'restaurant chain opening', 'new restaurant location announcement', 'restaurant franchise expansion' via rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: Clusters of restaurant expansion announcements signal optimistic consumer outlook, real estate availability, and labor market confidence—bullish signals for consumer discretionary and hospitality.

market_impact.rationale: Restaurant expansion waves signal consumer confidence, foot traffic expectations, and real estate market health.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS count for restaurant expansion announcements exceeds 10 for 3 consecutive days, go long XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count falls below 6 for 5 consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Quarterly earnings announcements and franchise disclosure documents release expansion plans in clusters; seasonal tourism and holiday planning drive announcement waves regularly.

title: Hospital Admission Surge Detection Via Google Trends
thesis: Spikes in hospital/ER search interest signal acute health crisis events (seasonal flu, contamination events, heatwaves) that precede healthcare utilization surges and pharmaceutical demand. Hospital admission spikes drive emergency pharmaceutical demand, clinical supplies, and diagnostic testing—all benefiting healthcare sector.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly interest for 'emergency room wait time', 'hospital admission process', 'urgent care near me' via google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly search index exceeds 130% of 52-week median across 3+ major metros, go long XLV
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search index exceeds 130% of 52-week median across 3+ major metros, go long XLV Exit: After 3 weeks or if search index normalized to <110% of median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly interest for 'emergency room wait time', 'hospital admission process', 'urgent care near me' via google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal illness cycles, heatwave events, and contamination incidents trigger hospital search spikes multiple times per year, especially during winter and summer peaks.

schema.idea: Hospital Admission Surge Detection via Google Trends

data.source: Google Trends weekly interest for 'emergency room wait time', 'hospital admission process', 'urgent care near me' via google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in hospital/ER search interest signal acute health crisis events (seasonal flu, contamination events, heatwaves) that precede healthcare utilization surges and pharmaceutical demand.

market_impact.rationale: Hospital admission spikes drive emergency pharmaceutical demand, clinical supplies, and diagnostic testing—all benefiting healthcare sector.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search index exceeds 130% of 52-week median across 3+ major metros, go long XLV
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if search index normalized to <110% of median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal illness cycles, heatwave events, and contamination incidents trigger hospital search spikes multiple times per year, especially during winter and summer peaks.

title: Google Trends Surge In Pest Control Failure Searches
thesis: Spikes in pest control search interest signal housing stock deterioration, seasonal humidity/warmth events, or urban sanitation breakdown—all leading indicators of local property stress and rental market weakness. Pest control search surges correlate with deferred maintenance, rental unit degradation, and downward property value pressure.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly interest for 'cockroach infestation', 'bed bug removal', 'rat exterminator' in major metros via google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly search index for any major metro exceeds 150% of 52-week median, short XLRE
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search index for any major metro exceeds 150% of 52-week median, short XLRE Exit: After 4 weeks or if search index falls back below 110% of median
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly interest for 'cockroach infestation', 'bed bug removal', 'rat exterminator' in major metros via google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature swings and humidity cycles trigger pest searches monthly; urban deterioration and seasonal transitions ensure regular threshold breaches.

schema.idea: Google Trends Surge in 'Pest Control Failure' Searches

data.source: Google Trends weekly interest for 'cockroach infestation', 'bed bug removal', 'rat exterminator' in major metros via google_trends adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in pest control search interest signal housing stock deterioration, seasonal humidity/warmth events, or urban sanitation breakdown—all leading indicators of local property stress and rental market weakness.

market_impact.rationale: Pest control search surges correlate with deferred maintenance, rental unit degradation, and downward property value pressure.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search index for any major metro exceeds 150% of 52-week median, short XLRE
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if search index falls back below 110% of median

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal temperature swings and humidity cycles trigger pest searches monthly; urban deterioration and seasonal transitions ensure regular threshold breaches.

title: Tech Layoff Announcement Velocity
thesis: Spikes in tech layoff announcement frequency signal macro weakness, margin compression, and capital allocation collapse—driving sector rotation away from high-growth tech toward value. Tech layoff waves precede earnings misses, valuation compression, and reduced enterprise IT spending.
data_sources: RSS feed count for 'technology company layoff announcement', 'tech sector hiring freeze', 'software engineer severance' via rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for layoff announcements exceeds 12 for 2 consecutive days, short XLK
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for layoff announcements exceeds 12 for 2 consecutive days, short XLK Exit: After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count remains below 6 for 10 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count for 'technology company layoff announcement', 'tech sector hiring freeze', 'software engineer severance' via rss_count adapter via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Tech sector earnings cycles, macro recessions, and strategic restructuring announcements generate layoff news regularly; thresholds breach multiple times per quarter.

data.source: RSS feed count for 'technology company layoff announcement', 'tech sector hiring freeze', 'software engineer severance' via rss_count adapter

schema.behavior: Spikes in tech layoff announcement frequency signal macro weakness, margin compression, and capital allocation collapse—driving sector rotation away from high-growth tech toward value.

market_impact.rationale: Tech layoff waves precede earnings misses, valuation compression, and reduced enterprise IT spending.

trade_logic.entry: If daily RSS count for layoff announcements exceeds 12 for 2 consecutive days, short XLK
trade_logic.exit: After 3 weeks or if daily RSS count remains below 6 for 10 consecutive days

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Tech sector earnings cycles, macro recessions, and strategic restructuring announcements generate layoff news regularly; thresholds breach multiple times per quarter.

title: Small Package Delivery Rss Volume Surge
thesis: Sustained RSS mention spikes for delivery delays signal capacity constraints, labor shortages, or seasonal volume compression in parcel logistics, pressuring shipping and retail margins. Delivery delays reduce consumer satisfaction, compress retail margins, and signal e-commerce logistics stress.
data_sources: RSS feed count for 'UPS delivery delay', 'FedEx package delay', 'Amazon logistics slowdown' via rss_count adapter
signal_logic: If daily RSS count for delivery delay articles exceeds 15 for 3 consecutive days, short XLY
entry_exit: Entry: If daily RSS count for delivery delay articles exceeds 15 for 3 consecutive days, short XLY Exit: After 4 weeks or if daily RSS count falls below 8 for 5 consecutive days
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use RSS feed count for 'UPS delivery delay', 'FedEx package delay', 'Amazon logistics slowdown' via rss_count adapter via scrape (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Holiday shopping, seasonal weather events, and labor staffing issues trigger delivery delay articles regularly; RSS spikes occur every few weeks during peak seasons.

title: Ev Charger Density Surge In Sunbelt States
thesis: Rapid expansion of EV charging infrastructure signals accelerating capital deployment and grid stress in high-growth regions, often preceding utility rate pressure and industrial construction demand. Infrastructure buildout drives industrial demand for construction, electrical equipment, and smart grid technology.
data_sources: OpenChargeMap API for daily EV charger count changes in TX, FL, AZ, NV regions
signal_logic: If weekly charger additions exceed 200-unit threshold in any tracked Sunbelt metro, enter long
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly charger additions exceed 200-unit threshold in any tracked Sunbelt metro, enter long Exit: After 3 weeks or if weekly additions drop below 100 units
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use OpenChargeMap API for daily EV charger count changes in TX, FL, AZ, NV regions via api (daily).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: EV charger deployment accelerates seasonally in Q1 and Q2; regional clusters frequently hit thresholds as utilities and private firms push infrastructure rollout.

title: Construction Equipment Rental Search Surge
thesis: Spikes in construction equipment rental searches signal acceleration in commercial/residential development permitting and project starts, leading infrastructure and materials demand. Equipment rental surge precedes capital spending in construction, cement demand, and industrial equipment manufacturing.
data_sources: Google Trends weekly interest for 'heavy equipment rental', 'excavator rental', 'crane rental' via google_trends adapter
signal_logic: If weekly search index exceeds 125% of 13-week moving average, go long XLI
entry_exit: Entry: If weekly search index exceeds 125% of 13-week moving average, go long XLI Exit: After 4 weeks or if index falls to 105% of moving average
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use Google Trends weekly interest for 'heavy equipment rental', 'excavator rental', 'crane rental' via google_trends adapter via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal construction cycles, permit issuance waves, and weather-driven project starts ensure equipment rental searches spike multiple times per quarter.

market_impact.rationale: Equipment rental surge precedes capital spending in construction, cement demand, and industrial equipment manufacturing.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly search index exceeds 125% of 13-week moving average, go long XLI
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if index falls to 105% of moving average

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal construction cycles, permit issuance waves, and weather-driven project starts ensure equipment rental searches spike multiple times per quarter.

title: Natural Gas Storage Draw Reversal Signal
thesis: Unexpected reversal from net storage draws to net builds signals demand destruction or supply shock, triggering rapid utility and commodity sector repricing. Reversal in gas storage trends signals weakening energy demand, refinery utilization pressure, and margin compression.
data_sources: FRED series IMNRNSA (US EIA natural gas storage) weekly readings
signal_logic: If 4-week moving average of storage draws flips from negative to positive, short XLE
entry_exit: Entry: If 4-week moving average of storage draws flips from negative to positive, short XLE Exit: After 4 weeks or if 4-week MA reverses back negative
position_sizing: Allocate 5% per signal, max 20% total exposure.
risks: Data source instability, false positives, and regime shifts.
implementation_notes: Use FRED series IMNRNSA (US EIA natural gas storage) weekly readings via api (weekly).
reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Storage injection/withdrawal cycles flip seasonally and during demand shocks; reversals occur multiple times annually in response to weather and economic cycles.

schema.idea: Natural Gas Storage Draw Reversal Signal

data.source: FRED series IMNRNSA (US EIA natural gas storage) weekly readings

schema.behavior: Unexpected reversal from net storage draws to net builds signals demand destruction or supply shock, triggering rapid utility and commodity sector repricing.

market_impact.rationale: Reversal in gas storage trends signals weakening energy demand, refinery utilization pressure, and margin compression.

trade_logic.entry: If 4-week moving average of storage draws flips from negative to positive, short XLE
trade_logic.exit: After 4 weeks or if 4-week MA reverses back negative

schema.reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Storage injection/withdrawal cycles flip seasonally and during demand shocks; reversals occur multiple times annually in response to weather and economic cycles.
